Orchard Park Trailblazers.description: A street gang based in the Roxbury area of Boston, Massachusetts, primarily active since the late 1980s. The gang is involved in drug trafficking, robbery, and murder, and has been engaged in a long-running feud with the Columbia Point Dawgs. Law enforcement crackdowns in the 1990s and 2000s led to multiple arrests and indictments.

descriptions: Orchard Park Trailblazers are listed as rivals of Columbia Point Dawgs in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Orchard Park Trailblazers

descriptions: Listed as allies in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Orchard Park Trailblazers , Heath Street Gang, Academy Homes Gang, Villa Victoria Park Gang , Everton Geneva Grizzleys

descriptions: Orchard Park Trailblazers and Columbia Point Dawgs are bitter rivals engaged in a long-standing feud.
edges.evidence_quote: They are bitter rivals of the Columbia Point Dawgs . Both gangs have been feuding on the streets of Boston for nearly 30 years.

descriptions: Dudley Park is listed as a rival of Orchard Park Trailblazers in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Columbia Point Dawgs , H Block, Dudley Park, Mt. Pleasant, Ruggles Street

edges.evidence_quote: The Hammerskins were affiliated with the record label 9% Productions.

Houthis.description: The Houthis are a Zaydi revivalist and Shia Islamist political and military organization that emerged from Yemen in the 1990s. Founded by Hussein al-Houthi, the group opposes the Yemeni government and is backed by Iran. It has been a central player in Yemen's civil war, launching attacks on Saudi Arabia, Israel, and Red Sea shipping. The movement is designated as a terrorist organization by several countries.

descriptions: Houthis are a non-state ally of al-Qaeda since 2023.
edges.evidence_quote: Houthis (since 2023)

descriptions: AQAP fought against the Houthis during the Yemeni civil war.
edges.evidence_quote: establishing a presence across multiple fronts in the civil war against the Houthis

descriptions: AQAP fought against the Houthis, allying with local tribes.
edges.evidence_quote: AQAP allied with the local, predominantly Sunni tribes to mount a fierce resistance against them

descriptions: AQAP and Houthis are alleged allies since 2022.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies Houthis (alleged, since 2022)

descriptions: Al-Shabaab is alleged to be allied with Houthis, though denied.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Houthis (alleged, denied)

descriptions: Al-Shabaab and Houthis have a growing relationship involving weapons and training.
edges.evidence_quote: There is a growing relationship between the al-Shabaab and Houthis. The former has pledged to escalate piracy off Somalia in exchange for more advanced weapons and training. The United Nations reported physical meetings occurring between the two militant groups in February 2025.

descriptions: Ansar Allah is closely associated with Hezbollah and claimed responsibility for attacks on behalf of Hezbollah.
edges.evidence_quote: Ansar Allah, a Palestinian group closely associated with Hezbollah, claimed responsibility.

descriptions: Al-Qaeda challenged Houthi control in Yemen.
edges.evidence_quote: While Houthi control expanded to the rest of Sanaa, as well as other towns such as Rada', this control was strongly challenged by Al-Qaeda.

descriptions: Houthis view al-Qaeda as mortal enemies.
edges.evidence_quote: the Houthis view al-Qaeda as 'Salafi jihadists' and thus 'mortal enemies'.

descriptions: Houthis accused the Saleh government of using Al-Qaeda to repress them, implying a conflict between Houthis and Al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: The Houthis in turn accused the Saleh government of being backed by Saudi Arabia and of using Al-Qaeda to repress them.

descriptions: Houthis and AQAP had a ceasefire since 2022 and cooperated in attacks and intelligence.
edges.evidence_quote: Al Qaeda Arabia Province (AQAP) and Houthis had previously fought, but since 2022 they have had a ceasefire. As part of this ceasefire they have cooperated in attacks against Yemeni government, provided safe havens for each other in their territories, and cooperating in security and intelligence.

descriptions: al-Shabaab is an alleged ally of Houthis.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... al-Shabaab (alleged)

descriptions: Houthis and Al-Shabaab have a transactional relationship that deepened over time.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2024 a UN report states that Al-Shabaab and Houthis had a relationship that was "transactional or opportunistic, and not ideological", while a 2025 report states that their relationship was deepening and posed a threat to regional security.

descriptions: Hamas has been an ally of Houthis since 2021.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Hamas (since 2021)

descriptions: Harakat Hezbollah al-Nujaba is a non-state ally of Houthis.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Harakat Hezbollah al-Nujaba

descriptions: Hezbollah influenced the Houthis' ideology and slogan adoption.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2003, influenced by the Lebanese Shia political and military organization Hezbollah, the Houthis adopted their official slogan against the United States, Israel, and the Jews.

descriptions: Hezbollah provides training and strategic assistance to the Houthis.
edges.evidence_quote: Since established in 2003, Hezbollah's Unit 3800 has provided the Houthis training and strategic assistance.

descriptions: Hezbollah influenced the Houthis' adoption of their official slogan.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2003, influenced by the Lebanese Shia political and military organization Hezbollah, the Houthis adopted their official slogan against the United States, Israel, and the Jews.

descriptions: Hezbollah commanders were actively involved in directing Houthi attacks.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2024, commanders from IRGC and Hezbollah were reported to be actively involved on the ground in Yemen, overseeing and directing Houthi attacks on Red Sea shipping, according to a report by Reuters.

descriptions: Houthis were influenced by Hezbollah in adopting their slogan.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2003, influenced by the Lebanese Shia political and military organization Hezbollah, the Houthis adopted their official slogan against the United States, Israel, and the Jews.

descriptions: IRGC provided weapons and training to the Houthis.
edges.evidence_quote: In March 2017, Qasem Soleimani, head of Iran's Quds Force, met with Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) regarding the 'newly accelerated effort to empower the Houthis', including the provision of weapons and training.

descriptions: IRGC directed Houthis to strike Saudi oil tankers.
edges.evidence_quote: In August 2018, despite previous Iranian denial of military support for the Houthis, IRGC commander Nasser Shabani was quoted by the Iranian Fars News Agency as saying, "We (IRGC) told Yemenis [Houthi rebels] to strike two Saudi oil tankers, and they did it", on 7 August 2018.

descriptions: IRGC commanders were actively involved in directing Houthi attacks.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2024, commanders from IRGC and Hezbollah were reported to be actively involved on the ground in Yemen, overseeing and directing Houthi attacks on Red Sea shipping, according to a report by Reuters.

descriptions: Islamic State attacked Houthi worshippers, killing 142.
edges.evidence_quote: On 20 March the al-Badr and al-Hashoosh mosques came under suicide attack during midday prayers, and the Islamic State quickly claimed responsibility.

descriptions: Liwa Fatemiyoun is a non-state ally of Houthis.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Liwa Fatemiyoun

descriptions: Southern Transitional Council was an opponent of Houthis until 2026.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Southern Transitional Council (until 2026)

descriptions: Houthis are listed as an opponent of Islamic State.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Houthis

descriptions: IS lists Houthis as a non-state opponent, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Other non-state opponents: al-Qaeda, al-Nusra Front, Al-Qaeda in the Arabian Peninsula, Al-Qaeda in the Islamic Maghreb, Al-Shabaab, Taliban, Hamas, Hezbollah, Houthis, Kurdistan Workers' Party, Syrian Democratic Forces, ...

descriptions: Houthi movement is listed as an opponent of RSF.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Houthi movement

descriptions: SP88 was a unit that had allegiance to the Bambu Runcing Division.
edges.evidence_quote: This saw small units of the lasykar who had taken some allegiance to the Bambu Runcing Division through the SP88 to take up the characteristics of pre-war gangs.

descriptions: The PKK lists the Communist Party of Turkey/Marxist–Leninist as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ... Communist Party of Turkey/Marxist–Leninist

descriptions: The PKK cooperated with TKP/ML as part of the Peoples' United Revolutionary Movement.
edges.evidence_quote: In March 2016, the PKK helped to launch the Peoples' United Revolutionary Movement with nine other Kurdish and Turkish revolutionary leftist, socialist and communist groups (including the TKP/ML , THKP-C/MLSPB , MKP , TKEP/L , TİKB , DKP , DK and MLKP ) with the aim of overthrowing the Turkish government of Recep Tayyip Erdoğan.

descriptions: The 'ndrina of Franco Coco Trovato is allied with the De Stefano clan.
edges.evidence_quote: alleato dei De Stefano di Reggio Calabria

descriptions: Roberto Sinesi of Sinesi-Francavilla is linked to the 'ndrina of Franco Coco Trovato.
edges.evidence_quote: Roberto Sinesi, legato alla 'ndrina calabrese di Franco Coco Trovato (alleato dei De Stefano di Reggio Calabria).

Contini clan.description: The Contini clan is a powerful Neapolitan Camorra clan operating in Naples, Italy, founded in the early 1980s by Edoardo Contini. It is a founding member of the Secondigliano Alliance and is active in drug trafficking, extortion, and money laundering. The clan has a strong presence in Europe, including Spain, the Netherlands, and Eastern Europe. It is considered one of the most powerful Camorra groups due to its lack of internal splits and informants.

descriptions: The Contini clan is a subgroup of the Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: The Camorra's organizational structure is divided into individual groups called 'clans'.

descriptions: Contini clan is a Camorra clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Antonio Righi, called "Tonino il biondo", was considered by the investigators as a money laudererer on behalf of the Contini and Mazzarella clans in London .

descriptions: Contini clan is one of the clans holding real power within the Camorra.
edges.evidence_quote: According to Misso, the real power of the organization is now in the hands of the Licciardi clan, Mallardo clan, Moccia clan and Contini clan.

descriptions: Clan Di Lauro had a pact regarding drug matters with Clan Contini.
edges.evidence_quote: il clan Di Lauro aveva un patto per questioni di droga con il clan Contini

descriptions: Di Lauro clan has ties to Contini clan for drug trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: The Di Lauro clan also has ties to the Sacra Corona Unita and Ciro Contini, a member of the Contini Clan, regarding drug trafficking.

descriptions: The Giuliano and Contini clans fought a faida in 1984.
edges.evidence_quote: faida tra i Giuliano e i Contini : combattuta nel 1984

descriptions: The Giuliano clan fought wars against the Contini clan.
edges.evidence_quote: Le numerose guerre tra gli anni ottanta e novanta contro i Contini , i Licciardi , i Lo Russo , i Misso e i Mazzarella depotenziano però notevolmente il clan Giuliano

descriptions: Mazzarella was at war with Contini, part of Alleanza di Secondigliano.
edges.evidence_quote: la famiglia Mazzarella entrò in guerra con l'Alleanza di Secondigliano, in particolare contro la famiglia Contini del Vasto-Arenaccia

descriptions: Mazzarella clan fought wars against the Contini clan, resulting in deaths.
edges.evidence_quote: during the eighties and nineties the organization ended up involved in many Camorra wars, in particular against the Contini clan, which led to the death of dozens of affiliates including the father of the Mazzarella brothers, Francesco, killed in an ambush in 1998

Al-Qaeda in Iraq.description: Salafi jihadist terrorist organization affiliated with al-Qaeda, founded on 17 October 2004 by Abu Musab al-Zarqawi. It operated in Iraq, targeting U.S.-led coalition forces, Iraqi Shiites, and others. The group disbanded on 15 October 2006 to form the Islamic State of Iraq.

descriptions: The U.S. considered Al-Nusra an alias of Al-Qaeda in Iraq, indicating a structural link.
edges.evidence_quote: On 10 December 2012, the U.S. designated Nusra a foreign terrorist organization and an alias of Al-Qaeda in Iraq.

descriptions: Al-Qaeda launched its Iraqi regional branch, Al-Qaeda in Iraq.
edges.evidence_quote: In 2004, al-Qaeda launched its Iraqi regional branch.

descriptions: Al-Qaeda in Iraq is a franchise of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Nuaimi 'has facilitated significant financial support to al-Qaeda in Iraq'

descriptions: Al-Qaeda forces were embedded in Jama'at Al-Tawhid wal-Jihad.
edges.evidence_quote: In Iraq, al-Qaeda forces loosely associated with the leadership were embedded in the Jama'at Al-Tawhid wal-Jihad group commanded by Abu Musab al-Zarqawi.

descriptions: AQI was affiliated with al-Qaeda, indicating a cooperative relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: Al-Qaeda in Iraq ( AQI ) was a Salafi jihadist terrorist organization affiliated with al-Qaeda .

descriptions: Ansar al-Sunna is listed as an opponent of AQI in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Ansar al-Sunna

descriptions: Asa'ib Ahl al-Haq is listed as an opponent of AQI in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Asa'ib Ahl al-Haq

descriptions: Fatah al-Islam was linked to AQI.
edges.evidence_quote: The Lebanese-Palestinian militant group Fatah al-Islam , which was defeated by Lebanese government forces during the 2007 Lebanon conflict , was linked to AQI

descriptions: Hamas of Iraq is listed as an opponent of AQI in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Hamas of Iraq

descriptions: Islamic Army in Iraq is listed as an opponent of AQI in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: ... Islamic Army in Iraq

descriptions: Al-Qaeda in Iraq morphed into the Islamic State of Iraq after 2006.
edges.evidence_quote: Al-Qaeda's Iraq branch, which later morphed into the Islamic State of Iraq after 2006

descriptions: Islamic State of Iraq fought alongside al-Qaeda in Iraq during the insurgency.
edges.evidence_quote: the organisation (primarily under the Islamic State of Iraq name) affiliated itself with al-Qaeda in Iraq and fought alongside them during the 2003–2006 phase of the Iraqi insurgency.

descriptions: Jama'at al-Tawhid wal-Jihad is a non-state ally of the Taliban.
edges.evidence_quote: Non-state allies ... Jama'at al-Tawhid wal-Jihad

descriptions: Cherry Boys is a rival of Ghetto Boys per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Catford Wildcats (Anti Shower), Peckham Boys , Woolwich Boys , Cherry Boys

Lueders Park Piru.description: The Lueders Park Piru is a set of the Piru gang alliance, part of the larger Bloods alliance, based in Compton, California. Originating as the Lueders Park Hustlers, it was instrumental in forming the Bloods in 1972. The gang is involved in drug trafficking, robbery, and weapons sales, with approximately 140 members in 2005. It has ongoing rivalries with several Crips sets.

descriptions: The two gangs were in a gang war, with retaliatory killings in 1985.
edges.evidence_quote: Gang war with Lueders Park Piru

descriptions: Lueders Park Hustlers helped form the Bloods alliance.
edges.evidence_quote: The Lueders Park Hustlers ... was instrumental in the formation of the Bloods gang alliance in 1972.

descriptions: Butler Blocc Crips are rivals of Lueders Park Piru.
edges.evidence_quote: The house was located within the territory of the Lueders Park Piru, but was used as a "hang-out" by members of a rival gang known as the Butler Blocc Crips.

descriptions: Kelly Park Compton Crips retaliated against Lueders Park Piru for a killing.
edges.evidence_quote: The Kelly Park Compton Crips held the Lueders Park Piru responsible for the death of Stevens, and in retaliation, killed 30-year-old Don Turner

descriptions: Lueders Park Piru and Mob Piru became enemies after internal conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: the two gangs became enemies after the internal conflict resulting in multiple murders during the Death Row Records era

descriptions: Lueders Park Piru allied with Mob Piru during the gang war after Tupac's shooting.
edges.evidence_quote: the Lueders Park Piru sided with the Mob Piru

descriptions: Mob Piru and Lueders Park Piru were allies but later developed a rivalry with shootings from 2009 to 2013.
edges.evidence_quote: Although the two gangs were allies, a rivalry developed after a Lueders Park Piru member was murdered in April 2009, leading to a series of shootings between 2009 and 2013.

descriptions: Mob Piru and Lueders Park Piru were previously one gang but split.
edges.evidence_quote: the Mob Piru and Lueders Park Piru were previously one gang, until they split apart

descriptions: Lueders Park Piru is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Lueders Park Piru

'Ndrina Lo Bianco.description: The Lo Bianco are a 'ndrina (organized crime family) of the 'Ndrangheta based in Vibo Valentia, Calabria, Italy. They are allied with the Barba family within the local structure. Their illicit activities include public contracts, extortion, usury, and control of the healthcare sector. The group has been targeted by operations such as Nuova Alba and The Goodfellas.

descriptions: The Bonavota are allied with the Lo Bianco.
edges.evidence_quote: sono alleati degli Anello , dei Lo Bianco e dei Mancuso

descriptions: Bonavota is allied with Lo Bianco.
edges.evidence_quote: The Bonavota are allies of the Anello, Lo Bianco and Mancuso 'ndrina

descriptions: Domenico Alvaro of the Alvaro 'ndrina conferred the 'Medaglione' dote to Lo Bianco members, indicating a cooperative relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: Il pentito Andrea Mantella racconta che fu Domenico Alvaro, detto Micu Scagghjiuni, quando era in soggiorno obbligato a Vibo Valentia, a conferire per la prima volta la dote di Medaglione a membri della 'ndrina.

descriptions: Lo Bianco and Barba are allies within the Locale di Vibo Valentia.
edges.evidence_quote: I Lo Bianco sono una 'ndrina calabrese di Vibo Valentia alleata dei Barba all'interno dell'organigramma del Locale di Vibo Valentia.

descriptions: Lo Bianco expanded their criminal activities at the expense of Pardea, indicating rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: I Lo Bianco riescono ad espandersi con le loro attività criminali a discapito dei Pardea, la prima 'ndrina nata a Vibo Valentia.

Cártel de los Rojos.description: Mexican criminal organization that emerged as a split from the Beltrán Leyva Cartel, founded around 2009 by Jesús Nava Romero and others. Based in Guerrero and Morelos, it engaged in drug trafficking, extortion, kidnapping, and other crimes. The group was active until around 2022, when it was dismantled.

descriptions: Los Rojos is a splinter group of Beltrán-Leyva Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Rojos, en Morelos y Guerrero.

descriptions: Los Rojos Cartel were former enforcers of the Beltrán-Leyva Organization.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Rojos Cartel, in Guerrero. These are former enforcers of the Beltrán-Leyva brothers, apparently independent and dominating much of Guerrero.

descriptions: Los Rojos split from the Beltrán-Leyva Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: El Cártel de los Rojos (conocidos popularmente como Los Rojos) fue una organización criminal mexicana surgida como escisión del Cartel de los Beltran Leyva

descriptions: Los Rojos Cartel split from Beltran-Leyva Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: The Los Rojos Cartel ... emerged as a split from the Beltran-Leyva Cartel

descriptions: Los Rojos consider CJNG an enemy.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: ... Grupos Criminales : Cártel de Jalisco Nueva Generación

descriptions: Los Rojos Cartel is rival of Jalisco New Generation Cartel according to infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mexico , Jalisco New Generation Cartel , La Familia Michoacana , Guerreros Unidos .

descriptions: Los Rojos are allied with Guerreros Unidos.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: * Guerreros Unidos

descriptions: Los Rojos Cartel is allied with Guerreros Unidos according to infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Guerreros Unidos

descriptions: A different cell called 'los Rojos' split from the Gulf Cartel, but this is a clarification, not a direct relationship between the two organizations.
edges.evidence_quote: No se debe confundir con la célula delincuencial de los Rojos, que son una escisión del Cártel del Golfo.

descriptions: Los Rojos fought La Familia Michoacana over territory.
edges.evidence_quote: Las autoridades confirmaron que la causa del enfrentamiento fue por disputa de territorio entre Los Rojos y células pertenecientes a La Familia Michoacana.

descriptions: Los Rojos fought La Familia Michoacana over territory in 2015.
edges.evidence_quote: The authorities confirmed that the cause of the confrontation was due to a dispute over territory between Los Rojos and cells belonging to La Familia Michoacana.

descriptions: Rojos and Metros are rival factions within the Gulf Cartel, engaged in infighting.
edges.evidence_quote: The infighting between the Metros and the Rojos of the Gulf cartel began in 2010

descriptions: Rojos faction planned to ally with Los Zetas to end the war.
edges.evidence_quote: On 10 November 2014, a document from the Mexican government was released to the media and claimed that Los Rojos faction of the Gulf Cartel was planning to ally with Los Zetas.

descriptions: Los Rojos is listed as a rival in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Los Rojos

descriptions: Guerreros Unidos initiated a dispute with Los Rojos.
edges.evidence_quote: Finalmente en 2011, El Tilde funda Guerreros Unidos e inicia una disputa con otros cárteles emergentes del extinto Cártel de los Beltrán Leyva conocidos como el Cartel del Pacífico Sur, La Mano con Ojos y Los Rojos, organizaciones que desaparecieron posteriormente.

descriptions: Los Rojos had a dispute with Guerreros Unidos, as stated in the context of the murder of Gisela Mota.
edges.evidence_quote: El gobernador del estado, Graco Ramírez, señaló que el crimen había sido ejecutado por miembros del ya desaparecido cártel de 'Los Rojos', quienes sostenían una disputa con Guerreros Unidos.

descriptions: Guerreros Unidos splintered from Los Rojos.
edges.evidence_quote: It splintered off from another gang, los Rojos (“the Reds”).

descriptions: Los Metros and Los Rojos, both factions of CDG, were in conflict with each other.
edges.evidence_quote: No obstante, estos dos grupos estuvieron confrontándose desde finales del 2011 hasta principios del 2012, y se cree que Los Metros han tomado la cabeza de la mayoría de los territorios del cártel.

descriptions: Cartel de los Rojos is a faction of the Cartel del Golfo.
edges.evidence_quote: Los rojos comandado por Juan Reyes Mejía González líder del Cártel de los Rojos

descriptions: Rojos is a faction within the Gulf Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Mario Cárdenas Guillén, brother of Osiel and Antonio, is the other leader of Gulf Cartel and head of the Rojos, the other faction within the Gulf Cartel.

descriptions: Cártel del Golfo created Los Rojos as a criminal group.
edges.evidence_quote: A lo largo de los años, el CDG creó grupos criminales como Los Ciclones , Los Metros , Los Escorpiones , Los Rojos , Los Sierra o División Sierra , Los Ceros, Fuerzas Especiales Grupo Sombra (F.E.G.S.), Los Zetas , entre otros.

descriptions: Los Rojos are listed as an enemy in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: ... Los Rojos

descriptions: Los Ardillos has an escalating conflict with the Cartel de los Rojos.
edges.evidence_quote: recrudecer su conflicto con el Cártel de los Rojos

descriptions: Los Rojos is an ally of Los Ciclones per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Gulf Cartel , Los Rojos , Los Fresitas , Jalisco New Generation Cartel

descriptions: Los Metros and Los Rojos are rival factions engaged in infighting.
edges.evidence_quote: The infighting between the Metros and the Rojos of the Gulf cartel began in 2010

descriptions: Los Metros y Los Rojos fueron bandos rivales en la fragmentación interna del Cártel del Golfo.
edges.evidence_quote: La división tuvo dos bandos principales particularmente, unos apoyando al Coss (Los Metros) y otros fieles a la familia Cárdenas (Los Rojos) por el control máximo del cártel.

descriptions: AQIM listed Ansaru as an ally in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Non-state allies ... Ansaru (2012–2017)

descriptions: Ansaru is loyal to al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: "Ansaru" which is loyal to al-Qaeda

descriptions: Boko Haram and Ansaru cooperated in kidnappings.
edges.evidence_quote: They were linked to a number of kidnappings, often reportedly in association with the splinter group Ansaru

descriptions: Ansaru was a group that was part of Boko Haram from 2009 to 2012.
edges.evidence_quote: Group: Ansaru (2009–2012)

descriptions: Ansaru is a faction that split from Boko Haram and is loyal to al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: These were the "West African Province" which is part of ISIL; "Jamā'at Ahl as-Sunnah lid-Da'wah wa'l-Jihād" (Boko Haram), under Shekau's control; and "Ansaru" which is loyal to al-Qaeda and rejected the caliphate of al-Bagdadi, though it shares his disapproval of the "wide-reaching interpretation of takfir" of Shekau.

descriptions: Ansaru is an offshoot of Boko Haram.
edges.evidence_quote: The U.S. State Department designated Boko Haram and its offshoot Ansaru as terrorist organisations in November 2013.

descriptions: The Gallo crew was originally part of the Colombo family but later left and joined the Genovese family.
edges.evidence_quote: To finally resolve the conflict, Brancato and the bosses of the other Five Families negotiated an agreement in which Albert Gallo and his remaining crew left the Colombo family and peacefully joined the Genovese family.

descriptions: The Gallo crew was officially part of the Colombo crime family but waged war against it.
edges.evidence_quote: The Gallo crew, officially part of the Colombo crime family and led by mobster Joe Gallo. The crew eventually waged war against their own crime family and considered themselves as an independent structure.

descriptions: Gallo crew member Bobby Boriello transferred to the Gambino crime family in 1972.
edges.evidence_quote: Bobby Boriello – transferred to Gambino crime family in 1972

descriptions: The Gallo crew joined the Genovese family after leaving the Colombo family.
edges.evidence_quote: Albert Gallo and his remaining crew left the Colombo family and peacefully joined the Genovese family.

descriptions: After a 1974 agreement, Albert Gallo and his remaining crew joined the Genovese family.
edges.evidence_quote: Albert and his remaining crew to join the Genovese family.

descriptions: Gallo crew member Michael Rizzitello transferred to the Los Angeles crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Michael Rizzitello – transferred to Los Angeles crime family

descriptions: Gallo crew member Vic Amuso transferred to the Lucchese crime family.
edges.evidence_quote: Vic Amuso – transferred to Lucchese crime family

descriptions: Gallo crew member Nicholas Bianco transferred to the Patriarca crime family in 1963.
edges.evidence_quote: Nicholas Bianco – transferred to Patriarca crime family in 1963

descriptions: Gallo Crew is listed as an early ally of South Brooklyn Boys in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Italian-American Mafia , Tanglewood Boys , Ozone Park Boys , Gallo Crew (early)

descriptions: Southern Bikers MC is a Bandidos support club.
edges.evidence_quote: two members of the Bandidos in Helsingborg would be arrested and charged for attempted murder after police executed searches of the headquarters of the Bandidos the Southern Bikers MC support club.

descriptions: Los Cazadores is a cell related to the Sinaloa Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Cazadores together with Los Salazar, (cells related to the Sinaloa Cartel)

Spanish Cobras.description: The Spanish Cobras is a primarily Latino street gang founded in the 1970s in Chicago, Illinois, by former members of the Maniac Latin Disciples. It operates in multiple Midwestern states and has a strong presence on Chicago's north and west sides. The gang is part of the Folk Nation alliance and is known for its rivalry with People Nation gangs, including the Latin Kings.

descriptions: South Side Popes rival the Spanish Cobras.
edges.evidence_quote: the South Side Popes' main rivals are the Spanish Cobras

descriptions: Latin Eagles went to war with Spanish Cobras after a party dispute.
edges.evidence_quote: This was the beginning of the Spanish Cobra-Latin Eagle conflict.

descriptions: Latin Eagles were allied with Spanish Cobras as part of ULO and Folk Nation.
edges.evidence_quote: The Latin Eagles became enemies of all People gangs when they joined the prison Folks alliance ... On the street, The Latin Eagles were members of ULO ... and became Folks alongside their ULO brothers; the Latin Disciples, Spanish Cobras, and Imperial Gangsters.

descriptions: Spanish Cobras is listed as a rival of Latin Kings.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Crips, Dominicans Don't Play, Folk Nation, Gangster Disciples, Latin Eagles, Maniac Latin Disciples, MS-13, Ñetas, La Raza Nation, Simon City Royals, Los Solidos, Spanish Cobras, Tiny Rascal Gang, United Blood Nation

descriptions: Maniac Latin Disciples and Spanish Cobras had friction over drug territory, leading to conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: After fighting together as brothers for two decades, the Maniac Latin Disciples and Insane Spanish Cobras began having friction around 1994 over drug territory.

descriptions: Simon City Royals feuded with former allies Spanish Cobras in the early 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: they had begun to feud with their former allies, the Spanish Cobras

descriptions: Infobox lists Spanish Cobras as rivals of Simon City Royals.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Latin Kings, People Nation, Spanish Cobras, Vice Lords, Chicago Gaylords

descriptions: Spanish Cobras are rivals of Latin Kings, a People Nation gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: People Nation gangs (including Latin Kings)

descriptions: Spanish Cobras are rivals of Maniac Latin Disciples.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Maniac Latin Disciples

descriptions: Spanish Cobras were founded by former members of Maniac Latin Disciples.
edges.evidence_quote: The Spanish Cobras were founded in the 1970s by former members of the Maniac Latin Disciples.

descriptions: Spanish Cobras are rivals of People Nation gangs.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: People Nation gangs (including Latin Kings)

descriptions: Spanish Cobras and Two Sixers cooperated to form SGD.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1989, leaders of the Spanish Cobras, the Maniac Latin Disciples, and the Two Sixers met to establish the Spanish Growth and Development (SGD), a structured organization.

descriptions: Spanish Cobras listed as rival in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: People Nation, Latin Kings, Latin Counts, Spanish Cobras, Latin Eagles, Imperial Gangsters, Simon City Royals, Harrison Gents, Almighty Ambrose

descriptions: SGDs had good relationships with Spanish Cobras through drug sales.
edges.evidence_quote: Drug sales created good relationships with all the Folks in the surrounding areas, including the ... Spanish Cobras.

descriptions: Allied per infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Mexican Mafia , Sureños , Street Saints 13 , 18th Street gang , Lennox 13

descriptions: Venice 13 lists Lennox13 as an ally in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Lennox13 , PlayBoys Gangster Crips , Santa Monica 13 , 18th Street gang , Venice Shoreline Crips

descriptions: The Rocks Push fought with the Straw Hat Push.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang was engaged in running warfare with other larrikin gangs of the time such as the Miller's Point Push, Straw Hat Push, the Glebe Push, the Argyle Cut Push, the Forty Thieves from Surry Hills, and the Gibb Street Mob.
